# Source Map Visualization

> Visualize and debug source map mappings. See how generated code positions map back to original source.

This is an interactive web tool for inspecting JavaScript/CSS source maps. It helps developers verify that their build tool (esbuild, Rollup, SWC, Oxc, Terser, etc.) produces correct source map mappings.

## What It Does

- Shows generated code and original source side-by-side
- Color-highlights mapping segments so you can see which generated code maps to which original code
- Draws connector lines between corresponding positions when you hover a segment
- Validates mappings and reports diagnostics (invalid source index, out-of-bounds lines/columns, unmapped regions)
- Calculates coverage statistics (mapped vs unmapped bytes) per source file

## How To Use

There are three ways to load a source map:

1. **Upload files**: Drop or select `.js`, `.ts`, `.css`, or `.map` files. If you upload a generated file with an inline `//# sourceMappingURL=` comment, the tool extracts the source map automatically.
2. **Paste**: Paste generated code (with inline sourceMappingURL) or raw source map JSON.
3. **URL**: Enter a URL to a `.js` or `.map` file. The tool fetches it and resolves the source map.

## URL Format

Shared visualization URLs use the path format:

```
https://source-map-visualization.voidzero.dev/<encoded-data>
```

The `<encoded-data>` is deflate-compressed, base64url-encoded source map data. Shared links are self-contained — no server storage needed.

## AI-Friendly Endpoint

When you visit a `/<hash>` URL programmatically (without requesting text/html), the server returns a structured markdown debug report instead of the HTML visualization. This report includes:

- Original and generated source code with line numbers
- Full mapping table showing generated-to-original position correspondences
- Diagnostics for bad mappings (out-of-bounds, invalid source index)
- The raw VLQ mappings string
- Analysis prompts for debugging source map issues

This means you can paste any shared visualization URL directly and get the full source map analysis as readable text.

## Built-in Examples

The landing page includes pre-loaded examples showing how different transformers (esbuild, Rollup, SWC, Oxc) handle the same source code, so you can compare their source map quality.

## Source Code

GitHub: https://github.com/Dunqing/source-map-visualization
